question_text: A student has a spring, a hot cup of water, a moving toy car, and a torch. Identify all the different energy stores present and explain in which object each one is found.
correct_answer: Elastic potential energy in the spring (when compressed or stretched), thermal energy in the hot water, kinetic energy in the moving car, and light energy from the torch
explanation: Different objects store energy in different ways. A compressed spring stores elastic potential energy ready to be released. Hot water contains thermal energy due to its temperature. A moving car has kinetic energy because of its motion. A torch emits light energy. This question requires students to recognise that energy exists in multiple forms and to match forms to objects.
